_doc: Ω-SINGULARITY source-root registry. omega.py scans these roots in precedence order to build the unified persona+module registry. Each entry: {tag, root, kind, purpose}. Order matters — earlier in `precedence` arrays = wins on slug collision.

roots.purpose: Canonical AGENCY tree (this repo's parent). 87+ runtime modules + 339 personas / 17 domains.
Extended JARVIS_OMEGA tree — adds experts/, bridges/, jbr/robotics/, prompt_optimizer, semantic_router, multi_agent, tool_registry, vector_store, streaming, tui, sandbox, evals, llm_router, tracing.
highlights: runtime/agency/experts/ — 8 expert modules (chemistry, clinician, contracts_law, economics, mathematics, neuroscience, physics, psychology_cbt)
runtime/agency/bridges/ — blender, cadam, dobot, lyra2, matrix_wallpaper, metaverse
runtime/agency/jbr/robotics/ — robot_brain, motion_skills, vision_perception, world_model, ros2_bridge, simulation, rl_trainer
runtime/agency/llm_router.py · prompt_optimizer.py · semantic_router.py · sandbox.py · streaming.py · tool_registry.py · tracing.py · tui.py · vector_store.py · evals.py

roots.purpose: Live JBR mirror in OneDrive — newest persona authored mtime, includes science (3) + standup (1) extra domains. May be unavailable if OneDrive holds file locks.
roots.note: Hebrew-character path (מסמכים) — sandbox bind mount can fail when OneDrive holds file handles open. Fallback to AGENCY-LIVE.

roots.purpose: KIMI Agent audit + JARVIS_SINGULARITY snapshot. 30 expert subsystems, multi_agent_orchestrator, ReAct loop, multimodal output, local LLM/voice/vision/memory, VR.
GodMode multi-LLM Electron browser app (third-party MIT). Useful as a UI front-end pattern reference; not a persona source.
User's organized desktop archive — extracted projects, documents, Hebrew misc. Scanned for any *.md persona-style files; non-persona content (executables, binaries, personal docs) is ignored.

extension_policy._doc: How to add a new source. Edit this file, add an entry under `roots`, then position it in the relevant `precedence` arrays. omega.py picks it up automatically — no rebuild required.
